meaning: n.可怕(或可憎)的人或物,妖怪,精灵
examples: There was no Halloween bogeyman in the closet for one Brooklyn woman in New York—just a 7-foot-long python in her toilet.
But why has the oil bogeyman become less scary?
IN BRITAIN it is the bogeyman that comes to take away naughty children.
For this and other reasons, the bogeyman of deflation is really not a concern at all.
And the administration is not above creating a handy bogeyman of its own: the insurance companies.
